subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/rights/politics rebuilt from independent sources (IMF, World Bank, V-Dem, RSF, Freedom House, HRW, Amnesty, CPJ, TI, ICG); Tunisian state media EXCLUDED there, with economics admitted via IMF/World Bank.
A democratic reversal: the president's 2021 self-coup dismantled the post-2011 democracy, packed the judiciary, and jailed the opposition, atop an economic crisis and a stalled IMF deal.
Standout drags: democratic backsliding, debt distress with a stalled IMF deal, an anti-migrant campaign, and severe water scarcity; relatively safe streets are a mild offset.

economic.notes: Debt distress with a stalled IMF deal and central-bank deficit financing.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt ~80%+ GDP and chronic deficits, with a stalled IMF deal (subsidy/SOE conditions rejected) financed via central-bank advances and Gulf/Algeria support - a real distress tail. (IMF Art IV; World Bank)

monetary_independence.notes: The central bank pushed into direct deficit financing under fiscal dominance, with statutory independence eroded. (IMF; independent press)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: A managed dinar with no international standing and FX restrictions. (IMF)

inflation.notes: Off a ~10% peak, easing slowly to mid-single digits, still elevated. (IMF WEO)

banking_stability.notes: Fragile, with heavy sovereign-debt exposure crowding out private credit. (World Bank; IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Declined phosphates plus textiles/tourism/olive oil and remittances, with a chronic trade deficit and weak growth. (IMF; World Bank)

institutional.notes: A dismantled rule of law and a sharply declining press, with usable economic data.

rule_of_law.notes: The 2021 self-coup dissolved parliament, dismissed judges, and subordinated the judiciary - rule by decree. (V-Dem; Freedom House; WJP)

statistical_integrity.notes: Economic data is IMF-engaged and usable; political data is opaque. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A bloated wage bill, capable but strained and politicised. (IMF; BTI)

press_freedom.notes: A sharp post-2021 RSF fall, with a decree jailing journalists for 'false news'. (RSF 2026; CPJ)

political_social.notes: Consolidated one-man rule, a rights reversal, and an anti-migrant campaign.

political_stability.notes: One-man rule consolidated since 2021 with the opposition jailed - suppressed stability over economic-despair pressure. (Freedom House; ICG)

civil_liberties.notes: A sharp reversal of the post-2011 opening, with mass arrests of opposition, journalists, and activists. (HRW; Amnesty; V-Dem)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A transit country, with a 2023-onward anti-sub-Saharan campaign, desert expulsions, and documented abuse. (HRW; Amnesty)

social_cohesion.notes: Economic despair, mass boat emigration, anti-migrant scapegoating, and polarisation. (BTI; ICG)

demographics.notes: Relatively young but with severe youth brain-drain eroding the dividend. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: Drifting from the West, with border-spillover and migration pressure.

alliance_reliability.notes: Drifting from the West - an EU-migration-deal partner, Gulf/Algeria-leaning - transactional. (ECFR; ICG)

conflict_involvement.notes: No direct war, with Libya-border spillover and migration pressure. (ICG)

physical_practical.notes: Severe water scarcity and decaying infrastructure, with relatively safe streets the offset.

climate.notes: Water scarcity, drought, heat, and coastal erosion, but relatively higher readiness than the Sahel. (ND-GAIN; WB MENA)

healthcare.notes: Once a regional strength, now degraded and underfunded, with medic emigration and drug shortages. (WHO EMRO)

infrastructure.notes: Decaying and chronically underinvested amid the fiscal squeeze. (World Bank)

crime_safety.notes: Moderate and relatively safe at street level - a relative strength. (advisories)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low penetration against drought/seismic exposure. (reinsurer data)

summary: The cradle of the Arab Spring has reversed course: the president's 2021 self-coup dismantled the post-2011 democracy, dissolved parliament, subordinated the judiciary, and jailed the opposition and journalists. The economy is in debt distress with a stalled IMF deal financed by central-bank advances, and a 2023-onward campaign against sub-Saharan migrants has brought expulsions and abuse. Severe multi-year drought and water scarcity, a degraded health system, and decaying infrastructure weigh on the physical side, with relatively safe streets a mild offset. The outcome distribution is negative.
